description: YAML-Defined Plugin System - Replace hardcoded C# plugin classes with declarative YAML-based plugin definitions interpreted by a generic backend engine

userStories.title: Design the YAML plugin schema as C# model classes
userStories.description: As a developer, I need C# model classes that represent the YAML plugin schema (metadata, fields, HTTP config, steps, extraction, transformations, mapping) so the engine can deserialize and work with plugin definitions.
acceptanceCriteria: Create model classes for the YAML plugin schema: PluginDefinition, FieldDefinition, HttpConfig, AuthFailureConfig, StepDefinition, ExtractionRule, MappingConfig
PluginDefinition has: pluginId, pluginGroup, displayName
FieldDefinition has: name, label, type (cron/number/text/password), required, sensitive
HttpConfig has: baseUrl (supports {{fieldName}}), headers dict, cookies dict
AuthFailureConfig has: httpStatusCodes list, htmlPatterns list
StepDefinition has: name, method, url (supports {{fieldName}}), responseType (html/json), extract dict, validate list
ExtractionRule supports: regex with named capture groups (for HTML), dot-notation path (for JSON), transform (byteSize/decimal/integer/toString), countMatches mode
MappingConfig maps parsed values to TrackerStats fields, supports computed expressions (addition)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Write BJ-Share YAML plugin file
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to translate the existing BJ-Share C# plugin into a YAML file at plugins/bj-share.yaml.
acceptanceCriteria: YAML file saved at plugins/bj-share.yaml
Defines fields: cron, required_ratio, baseUrl, cookie (sensitive), username
Configures HTTP client with: baseUrl from field, Accept: application/json header, Chrome User-Agent header, Cookie header from field
Step 1: POST to user.php?username={{username}} — regex extracts uploaded bytes, downloaded bytes, ratio, BJ-Pontos from HTML
Step 2: GET to torrents.php?type=seeding&username={{username}} — countMatches regex for torrent rows
Step 3: GET to torrents.php?type=leeching&username={{username}} — countMatches regex for torrent rows
Auth failure detection: HTTP 401/403 OR HTML containing login-related patterns
Value transformations: byteSize (comma-as-decimal), decimal (comma normalization), integer (strip separators)
Stat mapping covers: ratio, uploadedBytes, downloadedBytes, seedBonus, requiredRatio, seedingTorrents, leechingTorrents, activeTorrents (computed)
YAML is valid and parseable
userStories.notes: Study the existing BjShare C# plugin code closely to replicate exact regex patterns and transformation logic.

userStories.title: Write Fearnopeer YAML plugin file
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to translate the existing Fearnopeer C# plugin into a YAML file at plugins/fearnopeer.yaml.
acceptanceCriteria: YAML file saved at plugins/fearnopeer.yaml
Metadata: pluginId=fearnopeer, pluginGroup=unit3d, displayName=Fearnopeer
Defines fields: cron, required_ratio, baseUrl, apiKey (sensitive, type=password)
Configures HTTP client with: baseUrl from field, Accept: application/json header, Chrome User-Agent
Single step: GET to api/user?api_token={{apiKey}}, responseType=json
Auth failure detection: HTTP 401/403
JSON extraction: username, uploaded, downloaded, ratio, buffer, seeding, leeching, seedbonus, hit_and_runs
Validation: username notEmpty → unknownError
Value transformations: uploaded/downloaded via byteSize, ratio via decimal
Stat mapping covers all TrackerStats fields including activeTorrents computed
YAML is valid and parseable
userStories.notes: Study the existing Fearnopeer/Unit3D C# plugin code closely.

userStories.title: Write Seedpool YAML plugin file
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to translate the existing Seedpool C# plugin into a YAML file at plugins/seedpool.yaml.
acceptanceCriteria: YAML file saved at plugins/seedpool.yaml
Identical structure to fearnopeer.yaml except: pluginId=seedpool, displayName=Seedpool
YAML is valid and parseable
userStories.notes: Copy fearnopeer.yaml and change metadata fields.

userStories.title: Add PluginDefinitions database table and EF Core migration
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a database table to store user-created and user-edited plugin definitions as JSON.
acceptanceCriteria: New PluginDefinitions table with columns: Id (GUID PK), PluginId (string, unique), DefinitionJson (text), CreatedAt (DateTime UTC), UpdatedAt (DateTime UTC)
EF Core entity class created
EF Core migration generated and applies cleanly
Repository interface and implementation for CRUD operations
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Build YAML plugin engine — HTTP client and request execution
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the core engine that loads a YAML plugin definition, configures an HttpClient, and executes ordered request steps sequentially.
acceptanceCriteria: Engine loads a deserialized plugin definition object and validates required fields
Engine configures HttpClient based on the definition (baseUrl, headers, cookies — all supporting {{fieldName}} interpolation)
Engine executes ordered request steps sequentially
Each step produces named variables accessible by later steps via {{steps.stepName.varName}}
Engine detects auth failures based on defined rules (HTTP status codes, HTML content patterns)
Engine returns TrackerFetchResult with appropriate PluginProcessResult (Success, AuthFailed, UnknownError)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the core execution loop. Focus on HTTP execution and auth detection only — parsing and transformations are in the next story.

userStories.title: Build YAML plugin engine — response parsing and transformations
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the engine to parse HTML and JSON responses, apply value transformations, and map results to TrackerStats.
acceptanceCriteria: Engine parses HTML responses using regex patterns with named capture groups
Engine supports countMatches mode (counting regex matches in HTML)
Engine parses JSON responses using dot-notation paths
Engine applies value transformations: byteSize (handles comma-as-decimal, various units), decimal (comma normalization), integer (strip separators), toString
Engine evaluates computed fields (e.g., activeTorrents = seedingTorrents + leechingTorrents)
Engine maps parsed and transformed values to the fixed TrackerStats record fields
Engine handles validation rules (notEmpty check → unknownError)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Replicate exact byte size parsing and decimal normalization logic from existing BjShare and Unit3D C# code.

userStories.title: Integrate YAML engine with ITrackerPlugin and plugin registry
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the YAML engine to implement ITrackerPlugin so it integrates with the existing IntegrationSyncService and TrackerPluginRegistry.
acceptanceCriteria: YamlTrackerPlugin class implements ITrackerPlugin interface
YamlTrackerPlugin wraps the YAML engine and delegates to it for execution
YamlTrackerPlugin exposes plugin metadata (pluginId, pluginGroup, displayName, fields) from the YAML definition
Engine integrates with TrackerPluginHttpClientFactory for HttpClient creation
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Build plugin loader — disk files and database merge
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a plugin loader that reads YAML files from disk at startup and loads definitions from the database, merging both sources.
acceptanceCriteria: At startup, reads all .yaml files from a configurable plugins/ directory
Parses each YAML file and registers it as an available plugin
Loads plugin definitions stored in the database (as JSON)
If a plugin exists in both disk and DB (same pluginId), DB version takes precedence
Exposes all loaded plugins through ITrackerPluginRegistry
GET /api/plugins endpoint returns all available plugins (disk + DB) with their fields
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Add YamlDotNet NuGet package for YAML parsing. Plugin directory path should be configurable via appsettings.json.

userStories.title: Build plugin CRUD API endpoints
userStories.description: As a developer, I need API endpoints to create, read, update, and delete plugin definitions so the frontend can manage them.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/plugins/{pluginId} returns a single plugin definition as YAML text
POST /api/plugins creates a new plugin from YAML text body; validates syntax; stores as JSON in DB
PUT /api/plugins/{pluginId} updates an existing plugin; if disk-based default, creates DB override
DELETE /api/plugins/{pluginId} removes DB entry; if it was a disk override, disk version becomes active again; if user-created, deletes entirely
Validation: reject duplicate pluginId, missing required schema fields, invalid YAML syntax
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add YAML editor page to frontend — plugin list and editor layout
userStories.description: As a user, I want a Manage Plugins page that lists all plugins and provides a YAML code editor.
acceptanceCriteria: New 'Manage Plugins' page accessible from the navigation
Lists all available plugins with displayName, pluginId, and source (disk/database)
Clicking a plugin loads its YAML into a code editor component (Monaco or CodeMirror)
'New Plugin' button opens an empty editor with a YAML template skeleton
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Install @monaco-editor/react or similar package for the code editor.

userStories.title: Add save and delete functionality to YAML editor
userStories.description: As a user, I want to save and delete plugin definitions from the YAML editor.
acceptanceCriteria: Save button validates YAML syntax client-side and sends to backend API (POST for new, PUT for existing)
Delete button with confirmation dialog sends DELETE to backend API
Displays validation errors from the backend if YAML schema is invalid
Success/error toast notifications after save/delete operations
Plugin list refreshes after save/delete
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Remove hardcoded C# plugin classes
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to remove all hardcoded C# plugin projects and registrations now that the YAML engine handles everything.
acceptanceCriteria: Delete TrackerStats.Plugin.BjShare project and all its files
Delete TrackerStats.Plugin.Core.Unit3D project and all its files
Delete TrackerStats.Plugin.Fearnopeer project and all its files
Delete TrackerStats.Plugin.Seedpool project and all its files
Remove plugin project references from the solution file and TrackerStats.Api.csproj
Remove hardcoded DI registrations from Program.cs
Update TrackerPluginRegistry to work exclusively with the YAML-based plugin loader
Solution builds and typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Only do this after all YAML plugins are verified to produce identical output. This is the final cleanup step.
